title: nephrolepis davallioides
intro: Nephrolepis davallioides, commonly known as the Rabbit's Foot Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health. The therapeutic actions of Nephrolepis davallioides include expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in treating conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which contribute to its therapeutic effects.

side_effects: Nephrolepis davallioides can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory issues due to the plant's spores.
